Merge multiple external model coefficient data.frames into one

mergeExternals.Rd

Performs a full-join on a list of external PRS/coefficient data.frames, producing a single wide-format data.frame suitable for use as the external.ss argument to preprocessS.

Usage

mergeExternals(external.list, drop.ambiguous = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)

Arguments

external.list

A non-empty list of data.frames. Each data.frame must contain columns CHR, BP, REF, ALT, coef. The list may be named — those names are used as labels for the per-model verbose output and stored on the result via the coef.names attribute.

drop.ambiguous

Logical. If TRUE (default), drops strand-ambiguous SNPs from each input before merging.

verbose

Logical. If TRUE (default), prints SNP counts plus a per-model alignment breakdown.

Value

A data.frame with columns CHR, BP, REF, ALT, coef1, coef2, ..., coefM, where M is the length of external.list. Rows are sorted by (CHR, BP). The result has an attribute coef.names mapping the standardized coef1, coef2, ... back to the input list names (or model1, model2, ... if the list was unnamed).

Pass this directly as external.ss to preprocessS with external.coef.cols = paste0("coef", seq_len(M)).

Details

Each input data.frame must contain exactly the columns CHR, BP, REF, ALT, and coef (other columns are ignored). The first data.frame in external.list to contain a given SNP (matched by CHR:BP) defines the canonical REF/ALT orientation for that SNP in the merged output. Subsequent data.frames contributing the same SNP have their coef:

  • copied as-is if their alleles match the canonical;

  • sign-flipped if their alleles are swapped vs the canonical;

  • set to 0 if their alleles conflict (e.g., canonical A/G vs model B's A/T at the same position) — a real allele mismatch is treated as "this model does not include this variant";

  • set to 0 if the SNP is absent from this model.

Inputs are validated for missing values: if NA is detected in any required column (CHR, BP, REF, ALT, coef) of any input data.frame, the function stops with an error. Clean each input before calling.

Chromosome coding is normalized internally: "chr" prefix is stripped, PLINK numeric codes (23/24/26) mapped to "X"/"Y"/"MT", and "M" normalized to "MT". SNPs with unrecognized CHR values (anything not in "1"\(-\)"22", "X", "Y", "MT", "XY") are dropped per input with a warning.

Multi-allelic variants (duplicate CHR:BP within one input) and, by default, strand-ambiguous SNPs (A/T, T/A, C/G, G/C) are dropped from each input independently before merging.

See also

preprocessS, preprocessI

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
eur <- data.frame(
  CHR = c(1,1,2), BP = c(100,200,300),
  REF = c("A","C","G"), ALT = c("G","T","A"),
  coef = c(0.5, -0.2, 0.1)
)
eas <- data.frame(
  CHR = c(1,2,2), BP = c(100,300,400),
  REF = c("G","A","T"), ALT = c("A","G","C"),   # 1,2 flipped
  coef = c(0.3, 0.4, -0.1)
)

merged <- mergeExternals(list(EUR = eur, EAS = eas))
merged
attr(merged, "coef.names")     # c(coef1 = "EUR", coef2 = "EAS")

# Pass to preprocessS
out <- preprocessS(
  target.ss          = my_gwas,
  target.ind         = "gwas",
  target.ld          = my_ld_panel,
  external.ss        = merged,
  external.coef.cols = c("coef1", "coef2")
)
} # }
